query: speed of light in vacuum exact value SI definition
answer: The speed of light in vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 m/s by definition since 1983. This value defines the meter as the distance light travels in vacuum in 1/299,792,458 of a second.

results.title: speed of light - Metric System
results.snippet: | speed of light in vacuum | *c* | m s-1 |. The numerical value of the speed of light in vacuum, symbol *c*, is defined to be exactly 299 792 458 when expressed in the unit metre per second, m s−1. The speed of light in vacuum, *c*, forms the basis for the definition of the unit of speed, the metre per second. The fixed numerical value of the speed of light in vacuum, *c*, is defined exactly:. Inverting this relation gives an exact expression for the metre per second in terms of the speed of light in vacuum, *c* :. The speed of light in vacuum, *c*, together with the hyperfine transition frequency of the caesium 133 atom, Δ*ν*Cs, forms the basis for the definition of the unit of length, the metre. Inverting this relation gives an exact expression for the metre in terms of the speed of light in vacuum, *c*, and the caesium frequency, Δ*ν*Cs :.
